title: How tiny pollen grains helped solve a brutal murder: The fascinating science behind nature's invisible crime scene evidence
summary_full: Tiny pollen grains offer vital clues for investigators solving complex criminal cases. Forensic palynologists analyze these microscopic particles to link suspects to crime scenes. A notable murder case in New Zealand was solved using unusual mutated pollen evidence. Pollen's resistance to decomposition makes it a persistent and valuable forensic tool. This silent evidence helps identify and locate regions, aiding in criminal investigations.
